Admission to this room is every 20 minutes (15 persons per slot). Last entry at 17:20.
The CAM Collection is constantly growing and currently consists of around 12,000 artworks. Despite the many national and international loans granted by CAM, a significant part of the collection is more often in storage that on view.
While in storage, works of art are kept in the best environmental conditions (temperature, relative humidity, and light); two-dimensional ones hang on fixed and mobile iron railings, so as to optimize the use of space.
The Open Storage provides visitors with the opportunity to see more of the Collection, while also accessing an area that is shared by them with museum professionals who can use it as a workspace, to inspect or research specific works. Museum goers may therefore acquaint themselves with the way a collecting institution functions.

“More than four dozen artists and musicians of different generations established their studios here, and the space also generated and hosted numerous projects”, notes the curatorial team, highlighting the vitality of the city’s artistic scene that emerged there at a time of great difficulty, amid the global financial crisis (2008-2009) and the intervention of the Troika in Portugal (2010-2014).

The MURMUR project is part of a programme of temporary exhibitions dedicated to presenting works by Portuguese and foreign artists, designed especially for the walls of the MACAM Grand Hall. In this second edition, curated by Carolina Quintela, Portuguese artist Isabel Cordovil (Lisbon, 1994) presents an installation in which she seeks to explore the tension between inherited identity and desired identity, between language and love, norm and otherness.
Cordovil's work investigates the politics of revisiting narratives — whether in myth, folklore, religion, literature, dreams or around the collective unconscious — and the new navigations or manipulations possible of them. Mainly through installation and sculpture, her work reflects a poetic language of playful metaphor, visual freedom and disobedience, while seeking new ways of belonging/challenging and celebrating otherness.

full_description:
For 27 years, he devised four proposals for the construction of a Palace of the City to crown the top of Parque Eduardo VII. On the 50th anniversary of his death, this exhibition shows lesser-known aspects of these projects which, although they never came to fruition, reveal his evolution as an architect and the urbanistic thinking he developed about Alto do Parque Eduardo VII.
A municipal architect for a decade (1938-1947) and on his own after that, the author developed several projects that changed the face of the city and gave it modernity and monumentality that are still present today, such as the first version of the airport.
The urban and landscape interventions in Monsanto Forest Park and Campo Grande are two manifestations of a civic action that aimed to shape mentalities, transform social behaviour and force local decision-makers to plan the city.
This exhibition, featuring objects from the Lisbon Museum, the Lisbon Municipal Archive and the Gabinete de Estudos Olisiponenses [Olisiponense Studies Office], also highlights the constant relationship that existed between different architects and the never-realised projects for the extension of Avenida da Liberdade and the Palace of Justice.
In addition to the unrealised plan to extend the Pimenta Palace – a long-standing ambition of several generations of leaders of the Museum of the City and the current Museum of Lisbon – this exhibition will also provide an insight into Keil do Amaral's profound intervention for a Lisbon that is, after all, constantly changing.

full_description:
The MUDE – Museu do Design presents the exhibition
Meu nome António. Photographs by Teresa Couto Pinto 1981-1983
, running from 4 December 2025 to 26 April 2026.
António Variações (1944–1984) – singer, songwriter, and performer – stands out for the originality of his voice, the inventiveness of his lyrics and musical compositions, and the way he dressed and presented himself, defying the conventions of his time.
The 85 photographs by Teresa Couto Pinto – Variações’ agent, photographer, and friend – portray the artist’s strong personality, creative freedom, charisma, and visual aesthetic. Alongside both well-known and lesser-known or even previously unseen photographs, the exhibition also features items of clothing and personal accessories, reflecting how he reinvented himself through Portuguese collective identity while drawing inspiration from the cosmopolitan reality of artistic movements and international sounds.
His distinctive hair and beard styles, the colours he used, and even his eye make-up form part of the uniqueness and timelessness of someone who will always be António Variações.

Lisbon Marathon
This marathon is already considered as one of the most beautiful races in the world and acclaimed by international media such as the Forbes Magazine, the Huffington Post and American Express.
You may walk or run in this amazing courses.
Marathon - 42K
The 42 kms distance Marathon sets off from Carcavelos and follows the Coastal Road (Estrada Marginal), firstly by the sea and then along the river until the finish line at Lisbon, Praça do Comércio.
Starting in Carcavelos and finishing at Praça do Comércio, this course is 100% sea and river side, providing to the runners an unique view along the race.
Half Marathon - 21K
The Half Marathon sets off from the
Vasco da Gama Bridge
and charts a 21kms course that also finishes up at
Praça do Comércio
. It provides the opportunity to cross on foot one of the longest bridges in Europe.
The 21km course, takes runners from the north area of the city to downtown Lisbon, crossing emblematic historical monuments and river side views.
8K
The Mini Marathon has its departure at the famous Vasco da Gama bridge and the finish line is at the Parque das Nações.
New Generation
Shorter distance races which are within the reach of all those taking part, whether running or walking, since the main objective is taking part and meeting people.

full_description:
Collection José Carlos Santana Pinto
As a discerning collector, he has built a singular body of artworks in which conceptual reflection prevails, alongside a particular attention to formal economy and an intense relationship between word, number, and critical thought.
The contemporary artworks now brought together at MACAM reveal different facets of this collection, marked by the coexistence of Portuguese and international artists, by the search for visual and conceptual dialogues across multiple media that transcend generational boundaries. True to his vision, the collector understands art as a space of questioning — “the beautiful must ask questions” — thus granting the selected works a force that is as much aesthetic as it is reflective.
With this exhibition, the Museum opens the way to a cycle of collaborations that, alongside the Armando Martins collection, aims to give visibility to other private collections that do not have a public display space, thereby expanding the sharing of artistic heritage with the community.

The avenues of the Parque Eduardo VII will be full of life for yet another Lisboa Book Fair!
As with all years, the Book Fair is a great chance to find out about new literature as well as discover antiques and unique works.
You can also find out what is happening behind the words, as many authors – especially, but not only, Portuguese authors – come to the fair to speak to their fans at colloquiums and autograph sessions.
All of this in one of the city’s most beautiful places, with the river Tejo at your feet.

Uma Brancura Luminosa is presented at
Teatro Variedades
, in Lisbon, from
14 to 25 January 2026
, starring
Ricardo Pereira
and
Sandra Barata Belo
, who also signs the stage adaptation and direction. The production is based on the homonymous book by
Jon Fosse
, Nobel Prize in Literature 2023, and is performed entirely in Portuguese.
The story follows a man who drives aimlessly until his car becomes stuck on a forest road and he gets lost in a snow-covered landscape. Alone, he moves through a stream of thoughts and memories in which past, present and future intersect, calling into question what he sees and what is real.
The performance is built on a text that avoids fixed meanings, leaving multiple readings open and allowing the audience to inhabit the experience in their own way.

The Museum of Lisbon – Roman Theatre is hosting the temporary exhibition Esa Minoría Inmensa [A vast minority], produced by the Consortium of the Monumental City of Mérida and the Mérida International Classical Theatre Festival, which focuses on forgotten performers from our past: the common people of the Roman Empire.
Behind the marbles, the conquests and the official accounts, a different and essential reality existed: the common people. Peasants, artisans, soldiers, traders, the enslaved and the liberated, women, gladiators, prostitutes, actors and foreigners composed the silent majority of the Roman population.
A minority in the legends, they were in fact huge in number and importance.
The exhibition
Esa Minoría Inmensa
[A vast minority] aims to give them back their voice and leading role.
Dates: 20 November 2025 – 01 February 2026
Venue: Roman Theatre
Price: 3 €
Grants access to the museum
This exhibition is displayed in portuguese and spanish
location: R. de São Mamede 3 A, 1100-532, Lisboa

full_description:
Forms in Space… by Light (in Time), a monumental light installation comprising of a complex grid of white neon lights, suspended in the Oval Gallery. Alongside these light sculptures, the exhibition also includes sound works, installations and videos, highlighting the complexity and depth of the art of Cerith Wyn Evans (Wales, b. 1958).
Cerith Wyn Evans began his career in experimental cinema. In the 1990s, he moved towards a more in-depth investigation of language and perception, combining influences from cinema, music, literature and philosophy.
In recent years, he has presented exhibitions in renowned international institutions, such as Pirelli HangarBicocca (2019), Centre Pompidou-Metz (2024) and the Museum of Contemporary Art Australia (2025). He has also participated in various prestigious events, including the Venice Biennale (2003, 2010, 2017) and the 4th Moscow Biennale (2011). In 2018, he was awarded the Hepworth Wakefield Prize for Sculpture.

At a time when cultural understanding and diversity are becoming increasingly important, this festival aims to contribute to the dialogue by offering a vision of contemporary arab societies, cultures and realities, far from the stereotypes of media coverage.

The story of the love between the prince and Odette, a beautiful young woman turned into a swan by a spell, and the deadly deception and dramatic plot merge with Tchaikovsky’s music to create a magical experience.

LAAF – Lisbon Art & Antiques Fair
, opens its doors once again at Cordoaria Nacional.
LAAF - Lisbon Art and Antiques Fair is an event with a long history. The Fair takes place, since its first year, in the emblematic space of Cordoaria Nacional, often being called “Cordoaria’s Fair”.
All disciplines of Art, from Classical Antiquity to the Contemporary, take part in this Fair. In order to ensure a high standard of quality in the pieces presented, before the opening of the Fair all the pieces are analysed by a committee of experts from the most diverse areas, with a keen eye to excluding those that do not meet the conditions for participation in this great event.
At the same time, we develop a set of parallel events with the purpose of creating synergies with other cultural agents.

The festival coproduces and presents Portuguese artists of different generations and is an important meeting place for arts professionals.
For decades, Alkantara has promoted and developed projects in the performing arts, in Lisbon and abroad, that look beyond political, artistic and disciplinary borders.

One of the masterpieces of classic ballet, full of romance and fantasy, this production takes us into a Christmas dream with animated dolls, evil creatures and an unlikely hero: the Nutcracker Prince.
With its enchanting narrative that fires the imagination and exalts the ability to dream, this ballet is also a fable of the Christmas season, of longing for a lost childhood and the shock of the reality of the adult world and the dreams of children.

Jardins Abertos began with the simple, focussed aim of bringing Nature and the city closer together. Lisbon's gardens are places that can encourage learning and, through this relationship, just like a forest we have grown and become known for opening the gates of the city's most beautiful gardens, revealing their flowers, trees and gardeners.
The Spring Edition of Jardins Abertos Festival will take place on May with a programme including guided visits, workshops and activities for families with selected themes aligned with the various cycles of agricultural production: harvesting, preservation, transformation, but also wastage, including presenting creative solutions and research gardens around the city!
Come and visit the gardens of Lisbon!

Lisboa Green Trail
An event for the whole family to enjoy the Green side of Lisbon!
The Lisbon Green Trail is promoted by the Associação Walk and Run with the usual routes: 22K Long Trail, 12K Short Trail with Canicross and the simple Walk with 7K, and for the little ones the Kids Race.
All races will start from Keil do Amaral Amphitheater and you will have the opportunity to discover the most winding trails of Monsanto Forest Park and the best views over the city and river Tagus.

Portuguese designers present their new collections and trends for the next Spring / Summer, during the Portuguese Fashion Week. Always a in a festive and creative atmosphere Portuguese designers and brands promise to amaze their audience by the audacity and innovation of their collections, promoting Portuguese fashion of the international fashion and design scene.
Created in 1991, the ModaLisboa project has set out to be the first professional organisation for the presentation of the collections by Portuguese fashion designers.
Revealing a growing international image, every year the festival hosts tens of thousands of visitors and hundreds of journalists, both Portuguese and international.

Hans Zimmer enters The Next Level - and takes Europe with him!
The multiple Oscar® and Grammy® winner returns to Europe's biggest arenas in 2025 and 2026 with Hans Zimmer Live - The Next Level. The show will take place in Portugal on 31 March 2026 at the MEO Arena in Lisbon.
‘The Next Level’ promises a completely new show, with innovative electronic soundscapes and a spectacular light production that promises to surpass Zimmer's previous shows.

Painter and ceramicist, with ventures into printmaking and tapestry, Devrim Erbil (Uşak, 1937) is a leading figure in modern Turkish art, where he is known for his poetic interpretations of Istanbul’s urban landscape, capturing the city’s rhythm and rich textures.
Istanbul and Lisboa – Soulmates links the two cities through symbols and forms defining their identity and endurance, united under the shared hues of red and green, in a magical connection between two historic European port cities.
The exhibition also celebrates the diplomatic relations between Portugal and Turkey, which were established in 1843.
Until 31st December 2026 - 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. (Tuesday-Sunday)

The self as multiple, the title of the third temporary exhibition of the MACAM Collection, presents works by portuguese and international artists who, through different languages of contemporary art, explore the notions of body, identity, and perception.
Rejecting the idea of self as sole and immutable, this exhibition proposes a reflection on the ontological questions of what it means to be, to exist, and how we present ourselves in relation to others. Bringing together works of painting, photography, installation, and sculpture, The self as multiple is part of the debate on the subject, its mirroring, and otherness.
Works by artists such as Ana Vieira, Helena Almeida, Horácio Frutuoso, John Baldessari, José Pedro Croft, Juan Muñoz, Júlia Ventura, Vik Muniz, Yu Nishimura, among others, are presented.

The Deolinda project, launched in 2006, was a genuine revelation (even a revolution) in Portugal’s musical landscape, particularly in pop and folk.
Ana Bacalhau was the voice of this aesthetic and lyrical approach, largely unseen until then.
Pursuing a solo path since 2017, she has recently released Mundo Antena, an album that “dares to be both record and radio, music and spoken word, new and old”.
In this concert, the audience is invited to experience up close a singular voice that sings of tenderness and resistance, tradition and innovation, the past and what is still to come.
“Because”, the singer says, “there are nights that aren’t just concerts: they’re places where we want to stay”. Agreed.

Come and discover Lisbon’s most original market. The Feira da Ladra, or Flea Market, which is immortalised in the poem by the Portuguese singer songwriter Sérgio Godinho, is held every Tuesday and Saturday in Campo de Santa Clara, behind the monastery of São Vicente de Fora.
Traditionally dedicated to selling used objects, you’ll find real gems amongst indescribable bric-a-brac.
The market dates back to 1272 and was held in various parts of the city before settling permanently here.
From here you can easily visit the Panteão Nacional and the church of São Vicente de Fora, so the best thing to do is save this neighbourhood for a day when the market is on.
Between souvenirs and bargains, you may just take the best memory of your stay in Lisbon from here.

Naruto Shippuden Symphonic Experience is a unique show that combines the most memorable moments of the saga with live music, creating a powerful fusion of image and sound.
Electrifying battles, intense rivalries, and unforgettable friendships are brought to life through a soundtrack that blends rock, pop, and traditional Japanese instruments in a vibrant and totally immersive atmosphere.
Longtime fans, new followers, lovers of the Number One Ninja, anime, manga, or just music will be transported to the heart of Konoha—the Hidden Leaf Village—in an unforgettable experience that celebrates the legacy of Naruto Shippuden like never before.

full_description:
The Lisbon Half-Marathon is back again.! Challenge your speed and enjoy a fast-paced run with a privileged view of the Portuguese capital.
Speaking in competitive terms, the course is flat and very fast, the start is at the tolls’ square of the bridge and the Finish Line is in front of the Jerónimos Monastery.
Circuit
Starting at 25 de Abril Bridge, the athletes run towards Alcântara, via Avenida 24 de Julho, and head towards Praça do Comércio and once they reach Campo das Cebolas, they reverse their route, this time in the direction of Avenida da Índia, with the Jerónimos Monastery as the backdrop. It continues along the Marginal Avenue, following the Tejo River, almost to Dafundo, where they turn round to the finish line, which is located next to Jerónimos Monastery.
Surely one of the most attractive races in the world!

Queer Lisboa's film and activities program meets the growing permeability of Queer Cinema to the world, no longer restricted to a look at their individual and community realities.
The program of the festival continues to be faithful to the essence of a minority that is distinguished by its form of desire, which has given Queer Cinema over the decades a huge formal and narrative freedom.
Also to better understand and think Queer Cinema, we organize workshops, talks, masterclasses and exhibitions that offer our viewers tools to better analyze it.
With a set of proposals that we believe are encouraging and innovative, Queer Lisboa will continue to accompany the surprising, irreverent and not infrequently avant-garde queer culture.

Perfect for those who are “curious of ear and fearless of spirit”, this event has everything, from old music to jazz, through to classical and contemporary music, and even a tour of installations, juggling and an instrument-making workshop.
BIG BANG MUSIC AND ADVENTURE FESTIVAL FOR KIDS Big Bang is an international project that began as a business with six partners from five different countries.
It is through this project that CCB / Fábrica das Artes has opened a venue where Portuguese artists can create new artistic approaches to music for kids and see their work recognised both in Portugal and throughout Europe.

The Italian Film Festival has become a major event in Lisboa for fans of culture and Italian cinema.
This is already one of the most anticipated festivals with the largest number of followers in the film festival scene.
An event that is highly regarded for the quality of its cultural ramifications as well as the enthusiasm and passion of its organizers.
Created in 2008, the festival was born and raised in Portugal. Based on the work of a team of Portuguese and Italian professionals, it is deeply rooted in Portuguese culture and society.
Its aim is to display works that meet high quality standards, whether they be established creators or from a new generation of filmmakers, whilst keeping to the tradition of bringing to Portugal the very best of Italian cinema.

The Biggest Hybrid and Electric Car Exhibition, returns to Lisbon on May, it will be in FIL - Lisbon International Fair.
The ECAR SHOW will feature national and international brands, including major car manufacturers and companies from the electric mobility sector.
Visitors will be able to explore a wide range of electrified vehicles, including hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric cars.
The event also showcases micromobility options such as electric bikes, scooters, and motorbikes.
As usual, one of the main highlights is the unveiling of more the year's new models, presenting the latest technological innovations in the automotive industry.
Several brands will host national premieres in Lisbon, giving attendees the chance to discover the most anticipated launches of the year first-hand.
In addition to the exhibition area, visitors will also have the chance to take part in test drives, experiencing various electric models directly on-site.
With strong participation from major brands and a diverse program, ECAR SHOW reinforces its role as a key event for those following the evolution of electric mobility and looking for more sustainable transport solutions.
For more information and ticket purchases, visit the official website:

FIA Lisboa is the largest craftsmanship and multicultural trade market in the Iberian Peninsula and the second largest in Europe.
Handicrafts, gastronomy, natural resources and cultural activities and tourism are some of the areas you can find at this fair, which supports regional development and local culture.
As well as promoting and supporting local cultures, the FIA also promotes the relationship between tradition and modernity, helping with the display and sale of traditional and innovative products.
This is a great chance to see a large range of products and cultures.

A mysterious book lies hidden in grandfather Agostinho's library.
Driven by curiosity, siblings Tomás and Leonor climb the bookshelf, but their innocent adventure spirals into a massive disaster.
Books tumble, pages scatter, and stories get tangled up, and in the midst of the mess, the mysterious book disappears.
To set the magical world of tales right again, Tomás and Leonor must set off on a journey filled with unforeseen challenges, characters trying to find their lost stories, and astonishing dangers.
On stage at the Armando Cortez Theatre in Lisbon.
Saturdays and Sundays at 3pm for families and Tuesday to Friday at 11am and 2pm for school groups (by prior arrangement).
Secure your place now!

The Millennium Estoril Open is the biggest tennis tournament in Portugal and the only stop of the ATP Tour in the country.
The Estoril Tennis Club welcomes tennis stars from around the world and visitors from all over who fill the stands of the Millennium Stadium.
Come with your family and visit the Smash Area to participate in various activities at the stands of the event's sponsoring brands.
Indulge in the food court with over ten diverse options, stop by the Kids' Place where you'll find loads of fun for the little ones to play and babysitting services.
At the Fun Center, we challenge you to pick up a racket and engage in the many games and challenges we propose.

MUSEU BANKSY aims to bring the artist's message to a wider audience, especially in cities where his work has not yet appeared. Art lovers in Lisbon can enjoy Banksy's street art in one place and contemplate his ideas on various political themes such as war, peace, individualism, greed, poverty, capitalism, consumerism, and hypocrisy.
The museum showcase 100 reproductions of Banksy's artwork through video installations, graffiti, canvases, projections, and murals that shed new light on many universal issues. Visitors explore through different rooms, arranged by country, traveling from the UK to France, passing by the USA, Palestine/Israel, and most recently, Ukraine, our latest addition to the collection.
A unique immersive experience offering a journey through Banksy's world.

Edmazia Mayembe – 15 Years of Me, an intense journey through the greatest hits of the Angolan artist's career, who for 15 years has transformed feelings into music and made zouk a universal language.
For the first time in Lisbon, Edmazia celebrates with the Portuguese public a journey of emotion, love and authenticity, performing the songs that have marked generations and conquered hearts in Angola, Portugal and throughout the Lusophone diaspora: “Alma Nua”, “Karma”, “Se Eu Soubesse”, “Eu Quero Saber”, among many others, up to her most recent album “Emoções” which includes hits like “É Obra” and “Amanhã Não Sei”.
More than a concert, “15 Years of Me” is a celebration of history, culture and the strength of women in music. A night made of memories, new emotions and unique moments that will remain in everyone's memory.
One voice. One story. 15 years of music and love.

As part of the commemorations of the Centenary of Mário Soares, linked to the celebration of the 50th anniversary of the 25 April Revolution and the four decades since the signing of Portugal’s Accession Treaty to the EEC, the photographic exhibition “Mário Soares: A Man in Full” is both a historical and artistic record of one of the foremost protagonists of contemporary Portugal.
A unique visual testimony, the 50 photographs on display were taken over three decades by Luís Vasconcelos: during the revolutionary period, the consolidation of democracy and later, as President of the Republic. They reveal remarkable public and private moments of Mário Soares, capturing his many facets and expressions in diverse settings and circumstances.
Pavilion of Portugal Exhibition Centre

full_description:
From March 27th to 29th, 2026, the Técnico Innovation Center, in Lisbon, will become the epicentre of young innovation.
For 48 intense hours, hundreds of university students from across the country will work in teams to create technological solutions with real impact in areas such as Fintech, Mobility, Construction, Energy, and Connectivity.
The event brings together creativity, technology, and entrepreneurship, challenging participants to develop innovative prototypes with the support of industry mentors and leading companies.
The first day features the TecSummit, a space for inspiration and knowledge sharing through talks, showcases, and leaders from the Portuguese innovation ecosystem. Over the following days, teams will dive into their projects, culminating in a final presentation before a panel of judges.
TecStorm is more than a hackathon, it’s a stage for university talent and a celebration of the future of innovation in Portugal.
Learn more and apply at www.tecstorm.pt!

full_description:
The new exhibition at the Pavilhão Julião Sarmento, Art is a Matter of Consciousness, opens on November 4, Julião Sarmento's birthday, and is curated by Sofía Hernández Chong Cuy, internationally recognized in the realm of contemporary art.
The opening takes place at 6 pm and includes a performance by Joana Bastos and the highly anticipated concert by Dorit Chrysler. The exhibition will be on display until February 15, 2026, in Gallery 2, accompanied by a vast public program that complements the curatorial proposal.
The title of the exhibition, taken from a stamp created by Sarmento, supports an axiom that, although closed in its concepts, emphasizes abstractions that will dialogue with the artists, the public, and the museum space.
More than a conventional exhibition, the project is a living space for reflection, learning, and sharing.
The curator has brought together more than 30 works, not only from the Julião Sarmento Collection, and showcases works by Alejandro Cesarco, Alighiero Boetti, Céline Condorelli, Fernanda Fragateiro, Filipa César, Gabriel Kuri, Joana Bastos, Juan Araujo, Komar & Melamid, and Yonamine.

In an unforgettable night, the Hollywood Symphony Orchestra pays tribute to two legends of film music: John Williams and Hans Zimmer. Two of the greatest composers in film history, offering a unique journey that will captivate both classical music lovers and movie fans alike.
From John Williams we will hear the best moments of Star Wars, Indiana Jones, Harry Potter but also Superman, Jurassic Park, Raiders of the Lost Ark and Saving Private Ryan.
Meanwhile, Hans Zimmer will be remembered the powerful music from Gladiator, Inception, Sherlock Holmes, Pirates of the Caribbean, the Dark Knight Trilogy, and The Lion King.

Organised around large installations, the exhibition is defined by the rhythm they set and the connection found with other neighbouring artworks.
This display corresponds, in a fluid way, to a group of ideas that framed the choice of works: transgression (of the Portuguese dictatorship), manifesto (the first Portuguese artistic ecological manifesto), interiority (of the experience drawn from the artwork), mutation (technological, post-human) and evocation (of a real connection with the living world).
The majority of the contemporary works are in dialogue with a selection of modernist works, illustrating the time range of CAM’s collection and to create lines of tension between the different periods it covers.
‘Tide Line’ includes a variety of paintings, drawings, video, photography and sculptures.

However, her work has an incline for peripheral materials and states: for things, instances, or situations that we often dismiss because they appear to us as marginal or as escaping the utilitarian rhetoric that governs our daily lives.

Filled with emotion, identity and authenticity, the artist will perform her first album live, a highly anticipated work that officially marks the beginning of a new phase in her career.
With a career already filled with successes, Yasmine has won over Portuguese-speaking audiences with songs such as ‘Perfume,’ ‘Preta,’ ‘Noz Cantinh’ and the powerful anthem of affirmation ‘Esquece o Mundo,’ which has become one of the greatest milestones in her discography.
In this special show, Yasmine invites us to immerse ourselves in her artistic universe, where African rhythms, urban influences, life stories and an unmistakable voice intersect. The concert will also feature surprise guests, moments of great emotion and a production designed down to the last detail to offer an unforgettable experience.

Led by Diego Baliardo, a founding member of the original group, and accompanied by Canut Reyes, this is the only project that brings together two members of the original Gipsy Kings lineup. Together, they revive the authentic essence that marked generations, bringing to the stage the vibrant energy of guitars, gypsy harmonies, and rhythms that defined the identity of the Gipsy Kings.
“A Tu Vera,” released more than three decades ago, is more than just a song: it is a symbol of the joy, freedom, and gypsy passion that the artists have spread throughout the world. With its infectious melody and unforgettable chorus, it has become a classic that crosses borders and generations, remaining as current and engaging as it was when it was released.
The audience will also be able to enjoy other timeless classics such as “Bamboleo,” “Djobi Djoba,” and “Volare,” which have led the Gipsy Kings to sell over 60 million albums. The current lineup, faithful to family and musical tradition, keeps alive the cultural legacy and charisma that has always distinguished the group.
The “A Tu Vera” Tour 2026 promises two unforgettable nights at the Coliseu Porto Ageas on the May 1 holiday and at the Coliseu dos Recreios on May 10, celebrating history, identity, and passion in every chord.
Secure your place now for this unmissable comeback!

Amália
, whose life and work continue to inspire artists and visitors alike.

.
The first immersive biographical story dedicated to a national personality.
A journey of emotions hand in hand with one of the most inspiring women of all time. A dynamic and everlasting experience that will remain in the memory, beyond the boundaries of time and space.
An experience that only makes sense to us: sensorial, alive. One that crosses all the senses and emotions - the sound, the visual, the virtual, the immersive and the unexpected.
Over 700m2, in the city's new cultural centre in Marvila, we invite visitors, guided by the fado singer's voice, to embark on a unique journey through the artist's material and immaterial estate, scenographic installations, cutting-edge technology, dynamic and interactive content, combined with a storytelling that combines the physical with the digital.
In the eight rooms of this exhibition, we combine different thematic environments, where you can get to know Amália in her various dimensions - artistic, social, personal and international.
What you'll find
Virtual Reality
Crossing a high-impact scenic installation with a virtual reality experience, the physical and digital dimensions come together to provide a unique experience in which you can live the Amália of yesterday, today and forever.
360º Room
In this room we get to know Amália through an immersive 360º experience, using multimedia and videomapping, allowing the viewer to feel part of this "Strange Way of Life".
Hologram
With a full-scale hologram and scenography that replicates the Olympia stage in Paris, the public will have the unique opportunity to witness a performance by the fado singer.
Ah, Amália - Living Experience brings together SP Entertainment and the Amália Rodrigues Foundation for the voice of a culture.  This exhibition would not be possible without the inexhaustible support of the Amália Rodrigues Foundation, whose mission is to promote and publicise Amália's contribution to the affirmation of national identity.

2025 marks the 90th anniversary of Fernando Pessoa's death.
Casa Fernando Pessoa is marking the date with a new temporary exhibition, The Eternal Ark: Fernando Pessoa and Posterity.
The exhibition, curated by Antonio Sáez Delgado and designed by Ilhas Studio, invites visitors to reflect on the author's ‘lives’ after 1935: from biographies that reveal how the fascination with Pessoa continues to inspire new readings, to the editorial work that shaped the reception of his work, including the translations and international publications that established him as a universal writer.
Visitors can touch  books, watch film excerpts that transport them back in time, and take home posters or reproductions of Pessoa's personal documents. A symbolic gesture that extends the author's legacy and transforms each visitor into a guardian of his work.

In her first solo exhibition in Portugal, Sedira returns to the themes of art and resistance (and revolution), starting from an investigation into the Pan-African Festival of Algiers (PANAF) in 1969, organised by the new Algerian state, independent since 1962. Its capital, Algiers, was emerging as a place of ‘revolutionary’ encounters for many of the global liberation movements and the militancies and utopias of the 1960s and 70s.
The first Cinématheque on the African continent was created in Algiers in 1965, hand in hand with the emergence of militant cinema, with international co-productions.
Produced by the Algerian state, the William Klein’s film ‘The Pan-African Festival of Algiers’ (1969) is one of the key presences and evocations in the installation.

Connections is the inaugural exhibition of the Albuquerque Collection in its new home.
Consisting of almost three hundred pieces from different times, in different styles and techniques, it illuminates the multiple connections that the production of and trade in ceramics, particularly porcelain, fostered between very distant peoples and cultures across the centuries.
For centuries the Chinese were the best ceramists in the world. From China, their pieces reached far-flung corners of the planet, conveying a language of colours, motifs and forms with a profound impact on the ceramic traditions across the world. Chinese ceramic production, especially their unique proprietary porcelain, also reflected a complex network of external influences shaped by global trade links.(

“Children of All… Children of Whom? The Foundlings of the Wheel of Lisbon” is the exhibition that will be on display at the Museum of São Roque, from 17 November 2025 to 29 March 2026.
This exhibition will showcase a selection of the 90,000 foundling tokens preserved in the institution’s Historical Archive and is a part of the nomination of these documents for inclusion in UNESCO’s Memory of the World Register.
The Historical Archive of the Santa Casa da Misericórdia de Lisboa preserves nearly 90,000 foundling tokens — documents and other objects that accompanied the children left in the foundling wheel — organized into five documentary series dating from 1658 to 1939.
This is the largest collection of its kind in the world and soon a selection of these tokens will be featured in the exhibition “Children of All… Children of Whom? The Foundlings of the Wheel of Lisbon”.

The exhibition pencil for painting grey days – Works from the EDP Foundation Art Collection brings together works by 21 Portuguese artists. The title is based on a work by Carlos Nogueira, dated 1973, which includes a small card with the following typed phrase:
WHEN YOU GAVE ME A COLOURED PENCIL – RED –
WHICH WROTE IN PINK
A PENCIL FOR PAINTING GREY DAYS
It comprises six rooms, four of which are dedicated to as many artists, featuring exclusively works by Carlos Nogueira, Maria José Oliveira, Tomás Colaço and Luísa Correia Pereira. In the remaining two, there are works by Ana Pérez-Quiroga, Ângelo de Sousa, Eduardo Batarda, Eduardo Gageiro, Helena Almeida, Jorge Martins, Jorge Pinheiro, José Loureiro, Luisa Cunha, Manuela Marques, Maria Beatriz, Maria Pia Oliveira, Pedro Gomes, René Bertholo, Rita Magalhães, Rodrigo Oliveira and Tiago Batista. These works share a common idea of generosity, ‘capable of transforming moments and sometimes even days,’ says curator Margarida Almeida Chantre.
The EDP Foundation Art Collection was launched in 2000 with the aim of covering several generations of contemporary Portuguese artists, as well as various areas and disciplines of artistic creation. It currently comprises around 2,540 works by more than 345 artists. Its chronological starting point was the 1960s, a period marked by artistic ruptures which also symbolically corresponds to the date of the creation of the Companhia Portuguesa de Eletricidade, the predecessor of the EDP group.

​Partnership between MUDE – Museu do Design and the Lisbon Architecture Triennale
MUDE is once again participating in the Lisbon Architecture Triennale, hosting one of the three central exhibitions of the Seventh Edition, How Heavy Is a City?
The exhibition 'Spectres' is part of MUDE's programme as it continues the museum's ongoing focus on the needs of Planet Earth, questioning the ways in which we inhabit territories and the need to respect all forms of life. It is urgent to move from reflection and debate to the implementation of ideas that consider the importance of living in synergy with the Ecosphere.
The ‘Spectres’ exhibition presents multidisciplinary proposals that reflect the transformative potential of collective creative processes and experimental research currently underway, where design and architecture, contemporary art, history, science and emerging technologies come together and expand, seeking answers to questions such as: ‘What is a natural landscape?’, ‘How can we rethink water as a common good?’, ‘What is the sound of the Earth?’, ‘Where is sea level?’, ‘How can we welcome alien ecology?’ or ‘How fragile are infrastructures?’.
Thus, with this exhibition, MUDE's programme reinforces the dissemination of different expressions of design as a discipline of project, showing, in this case, the contribution of speculative design to the definition and implementation of new ideas with a view to building a more just and sustainable future.
Trienal de Arquitectura de Lisboa

Made up of almost 30 trillion tonnes of materials, global cities are an entanglement of constantly evolving structures, geometrically complex systems and spatially disperse environments. They are devices that shape energy, information and material fluxes, which accumulate over time and merge into the rigid exoskeleton of human societies.
What does it mean for architecture to be the support of these transformations? This dynamic process can be approached by investigating the material basis of architecture: its intricate constructions, foundations, frameworks, cracks, models, joints, erosion, wear and tear, renovations and interconnections. The question of finding out how to measure the weight of a city challenges the public to detect signals of the Anthropocene amid the noise of the Earth.
Fluxes is one of the three main exhibitions of 'How Heavy Is a City?', the 7th edition of the Lisbon Architecture Triennale, together with Spectres, at MUDE – Museu do Design, and Lighter, at MAC/CCB.

The exhibition ‘Francisco Sá Carneiro and the Construction of Portuguese Democracy’ addresses the public life of Francisco Sá Carneiro, from his origins to his death, establishing itself in three major temporal and documentary centres: before 25 April, from the revolutionary period to the consolidation of the democratic regime, and finally, the political legacy of the founder of the PPD.
With this chronological narrative, the exhibition presents unpublished documents belonging to the Ephemera Archive. These are mostly manuscripts, correspondence, meeting notes, personal diaries, photocopies, books, among others. Original iconographic materials, posters, drawings, stickers, objects and flags will also be on display.
